title: Tools, Device Commands, PBQs, and Certification Capstone · Fault Triage
summary: Use software, hardware, discovery, capture, and device evidence to design, implement, secure, operate, and repair a branch network. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a performance-based branch network capstone and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Use ping, traceroute or tracert, nslookup, dig, netstat, ip, ifconfig, ipconfig, arp, and tcpdump with platform-aware interpretation.
Use protocol analyzers and authorized Nmap discovery or service probes with scope, timing, vantage, and false-result awareness.
Use LLDP or CDP and speed tests while accounting for device support, advertisement trust, test path, server, and competing load.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Net Certification Evidence evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Net Certification Evidence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network tools capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Certification Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a performance-based branch network capstone while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Memorizing acronyms and port numbers demonstrates complete Network Plus readiness. This fails the Net Certification Evidence contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: CompTIA Network Plus N10-009 Exam Objectives (N10-009 v4.0) — Controls every certification domain, objective, acronym, tool, protocol, and performance-based scope decision.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives.

hints: Name the Net Certification Evidence precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed debug case derived from this module: network tools capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Memorizing acronyms and port numbers demonstrates complete Network Plus readiness.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: network tools capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-certification-evidence-17kgex" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Certification Evidence into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: net-certification-evidence-17kgex
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable probable cause]
# command: choose one safe read-only simulator command
# observation: [replace with the result that supports or rejects the hypothesis]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: Name the Net Certification Evidence precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Certification Evidence applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives.
